text:
*[Bug 19055] New: grDevices::embedFonts does not embed all fonts*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19055>

            Bug ID: 19055
           Summary: grDevices::embedFonts does not embed all fonts
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.6.x
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Linux
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Graphics
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:cdanek@posteo.de|cdanek@posteo.de>

Hi

The figure
```
plotname <- "test.pdf"
grDevices::pdf(plotname)
plot(0, type="n", main=eval(substitute(expression(paste(Delta, "pCO"[2], "
[µatm]"))))) # I need the substitute() for other cases
dev.off()
grDevices::embedFonts(plotname, outfile=plotname)
```
does not seem to have all fonts embedded:
```
pdffonts test.pdf 
name                                 type              encoding         emb sub
uni object ID
------------------------------------ ----------------- ---------------- --- ---
--- ---------
Helvetica                            Type 1            Custom           no  no 
no      11  0
Symbol                               Type 1            Custom           no  no 
no      12  0
```
(alternatively: `evince test.pdf` -> Properties -> Fonts)


On my system (tested multiple linux distros), the command to embed fonts that
is called by `grDevices::embedFonts` is
```
gs -dNOPAUSE -dBATCH -q -dAutoRotatePages=/None -sDEVICE=pdfwrite
-sOutputFile='/tmp/RtmpkdPC8c/Rembed662c878593840' 'test.pdf'
```
When I add the argument `-dPDFSETTINGS=/prepress` to this command, all fonts
seem to get properly embedded:
```
pdffonts test.pdf 
name                                 type              encoding         emb sub
uni object ID
------------------------------------ ----------------- ---------------- --- ---
--- ---------
DFBTYZ+Helvetica                     Type 1C           Custom           yes yes
no      11  0
XKEYMM+Symbol                        Type 1C           Custom           yes yes
no      13  0
```

This is important to me since I am using R to generate scientific vector
graphics that must look the same on every system, no matter which fonts are
installed on that system.
I don't know if this a general solution but to me it is.
